title: Crownmate RedLight Helmet™
description: Home-use LLLT helmet with 40 laser diodes. Put it on, sit for 20 minutes, done — no clinic visits.
fullDescription: The RedLight Helmet is for anyone who wants red-light scalp sessions without booking appointments or holding a device in place. Forty laser diodes at 650nm cover the whole scalp evenly. It weighs about 520g, runs hands-free, and fits into a normal evening — most people do 20 minutes, three nights a week. Plug in the adaptor or use a power bank if you're moving around the house.

productFaqs.question: How long before I see results?
productFaqs.answer: Most people notice less hair in the drain around week 4–6. Thickness and new growth usually take 12–16 weeks if you're consistent — three sessions a week, no skipping.

productFaqs.question: Is LLLT safe to use at home?
productFaqs.answer: Yes. It's low-level laser therapy at 650nm, the same wavelength used in clinic devices. Follow the 20-minute session limit and don't use it on broken or irritated skin.

productFaqs.question: How long is each session?
productFaqs.answer: 20 minutes. That's it. Three times a week is what we recommend — more isn't better.

productFaqs.question: Can women use this too?
productFaqs.answer: Absolutely. Works the same for men and women dealing with thinning or early-stage hair loss.

productFaqs.question: Does it work on a completely bald scalp?
productFaqs.answer: It helps most where follicles are still there but weak. Areas that have been smooth bald for years won't respond much — LLLT isn't a hair transplant.

title: Crownmate High Frequency Wand™
description: 4-in-1 high-frequency wand with swap-in glass electrodes. Scalp, face, neck — one tool, short sessions.
fullDescription: The HF Wand runs a neon-gas electrode system with adjustable intensity. You get four glass heads for different zones — scalp, spot treatment, broader facial areas. Sessions are short, usually 3–5 minutes per area. A lot of customers use it right before oil or serum so the scalp feels more receptive. Good add-on if you already have a basic hair routine and want something extra without a big time commitment.

productFaqs.question: How often should I use it?
productFaqs.answer: 2–3 times a week is plenty. Keep each zone to 3–5 minutes — you'll feel a mild tingle, that's normal.

productFaqs.question: Is high-frequency safe for home use?
productFaqs.answer: Yes, if you start low and work up. Avoid open cuts, active acne, or metal jewellery in the area you're treating.

productFaqs.question: Which electrode should I use?
productFaqs.answer: The comb-shaped one for scalp, the mushroom head for larger areas, the smaller points for spots. All four are in the box — check the manual for photos.

productFaqs.question: Can I use it with oils or serums?
productFaqs.answer: Better after a session on clean skin, or before applying product so your scalp is warmed up. Don't coat the electrode in oil.

productFaqs.question: Is it suitable for sensitive skin?
productFaqs.answer: Start on the lowest setting for 2 minutes. If it stings, drop the intensity. Most people with sensitive skin are fine on level 1–2.

title: Crownmate Pulse Pro Massager™
description: Rechargeable scalp massager with silicone kneading nodes. Waterproof, fits in your palm, works in the shower.
fullDescription: Pulse Pro is the everyday piece of the lineup. Soft silicone nodes knead the scalp in a slow rotation — feels good after a long day and helps work oil or serum through the roots. IPX7 waterproof so you can use it in the shower. Charge once and it runs for several sessions. Most people do 5–10 minutes a day, either morning or before bed.

productFaqs.question: How long should each session be?
productFaqs.answer: 5–10 minutes is the sweet spot. Longer won't hurt, but you don't need to sit there for half an hour.

productFaqs.question: Is it waterproof?
productFaqs.answer: Yes — IPX7. Fine in the shower. Just don't dunk the charging port underwater.

productFaqs.question: Can I use it on dry or oiled hair?
productFaqs.answer: Both work. A lot of people massage first, then apply oil so it spreads easier.

productFaqs.question: How do I clean it?
productFaqs.answer: Rinse the nodes under tap water, wipe dry. Comes with a cover for travel.

productFaqs.question: Is it suitable for all hair types?
productFaqs.answer: Yes. The silicone is soft enough for fine hair and thick curls alike.

title: Crownmate Serum Stamp™
description: 140-needle stamp with a built-in serum tank. Press, stamp, done — your growth serum actually gets into the scalp.
fullDescription: The Serum Stamp is for anyone who uses hair growth serums but feels like most of it sits on the surface. One hundred forty fine needles create tiny channels while the integrated 10ml chamber feeds product straight through — no dripping, no wasted drops. Twist on the stamp head, dial the depth up to 0.25mm, and stamp evenly across thinning areas. Most people do it once or twice a week right before bed, paired with whatever serum they already use. Rinse the head, pop the silicone plug back in, and it’s ready for next time.

productFaqs.question: Does it hurt?
productFaqs.answer: You’ll feel a light prickle, not pain. Start at 0.10mm for your first session and work up to 0.25mm once your scalp gets used to it. If it stings, go shallower.

productFaqs.question: Can I use it with any serum?
productFaqs.answer: Yes — fill the chamber with whatever hair growth serum or tonic you already use. Avoid thick oils that won’t flow through the needles; watery serums work best.

productFaqs.question: How do I clean it after use?
productFaqs.answer: Rinse the stamp head under warm water, let it air dry, and put the protective cover back on. Seal leftover serum in the chamber with the silicone plug.

productFaqs.question: How often should I use it?
productFaqs.answer: Once or twice a week is enough. More isn’t better — your scalp needs time to recover between sessions.

productFaqs.question: Does it work with the RedLight Helmet or HF Wand?
productFaqs.answer: A lot of customers stamp first, apply serum through the device, then do their helmet or wand session on other nights. It’s the prep step that makes serums actually absorb.
